scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal spa execution only when booking, room-turn, sanitation, and closeout evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing service-policy, contraindication-routing, and sanitation prerequisites produced a blocked exception state instead of improvised progression.
The lane refused completion without auditable closure evidence and preserved the open or rework state.
A med-spa procedure request was treated as an adjacent-lane boundary case instead of being normalized as generic spa work.
Potential treatment-suitability and adverse-reaction questions were routed to licensed owners without the operations lane inventing clinical authority.
A sanitation-log failure correctly triggered rework and customer-impact handling rather than silent room reuse.
Booking, room-turn, linen, and maintenance mismatches stayed unresolved and visible until the authoritative records reconciled.
Spa-specific terminology and artifacts such as service menu, treatment room, add-on, room turn, and late-arrival policy were preserved instead of generic service language.
Missing tenant facts and system mappings triggered low-confidence escalation instead of a confident but unsupported answer.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant policy signals were preserved as an escalation rather than synthesized into an unsafe unilateral answer.
Tenant-specific stricter room-turn, minors, and wet-area controls were applied without displacing the public sanitation and safety baseline.
A blood-exposure incident was treated as a worker-safety control case with refusal of schedule-driven shortcuts and correct escalation routing.

notes: Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for incomplete inputs, execution exceptions requiring rework or escalation, and refusal to mark work complete without evidence.
All required shared, execution-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
